meaning: adj.装饰的,华丽的,(文体)绚丽的
examples: The old Wilma Theatre is a funky, lovely, ornate riverside venue.
The ornate features of Buckingham Palace will be reflected on the cake, but most of the decorations will be floral, and each has a meaning.
The researchers mapped not only the city's vast and ornate ceremonial areas, but also hundreds of simpler apartment complexes where common people lived.
The factory's latticed windows and ornate working liquor distillery make it worth the trip.
Sometimes the hoods are made from simple leather, other times they are more ornate with studs and eyelets.
